markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Mark papers, GitHub projects, and external docs as non-normative research inputs and copy accepted technical substance into WP/spec-native fields. This MT belongs to ResearchSpecTranslation. Convert research, operator intent, and current authority into self-contained WP/spec-ready requirements.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Research papers and GitHub/codebase findings are context inputs only; translate every accepted idea into Handshake-native requirements before activation.
Activation must cite SPEC_CURRENT and the v02.192 indexed bundle as current authority, not the v02.182 monolith alone.
scope.expected_behavior: ExternalResearchNonNormativeBoundary leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ExternalResearchNonNormativeBoundary is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: AC1 (ExternalResearchNonNormativeBoundary specified for no-context continuation) -> the boundary is declared in .GOV/task_packets/stubs/WP-KERNEL-009-Project-Knowledge-Index-Loom-Rich-Editor-v1.contract.json research_basis.normative_status=non_normative_context_only and research_basis.translation_rule (papers, GitHub repositories, docs and discussions may explain why WP-009 exists but are not source of truth); all 20 research_basis.sources_checked entries (MemGraphRAG PDF + arXiv, Microsoft GraphRAG, LightRAG, Graphiti, Mem0, Letta, Understand-Anything, Karpathy wiki gist, Aider repomap, Sourcegraph/SCIP, Tree-sitter, Tiptap, ProseMirror, CodeMirror, Monaco, Obsidian backlinks/graph) carry only relevant_pattern context; accepted substance copied into WP-native fields translated_memory_system_spec, architecture_seed, hs_rich_text_editor and into applied spec law .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md#2.3.13.11

Failure scenario for sidecar reliance is explicit -> stub failure_scenarios entry (future model treats MemGraphRAG PDF or GitHub repositories as required implementation authority) with mitigation requiring WP-stored translated requirements and future enrichment into SPEC_CURRENT

AC2 (exact files, state tables, constraints, failure modes, verification evidence) -> exact files pinned in this MT scope.relevant_files and scope.constraints; state tables and APIs: stub architecture_seed.data_model_seed (incl. native_research_translation_requirements table) + core_components Native Research Translation Registry; failure modes: stub failure_scenarios; verification evidence: refinement.json refinement.verification_results (4x PASS, 1x BLOCKED_PRE_EXISTING)

AC3 (sidecar research rejected, ideas restated Handshake-native) -> same artifacts as AC1 plus stub research_basis.rejected_options (leaving MemGraphRAG or any paper as a sidecar dependency for future implementers: rejected); this MT scope.constraints bar sidecar-paper and outside-runtime proof

AC4 (HBR rows named with concrete reasons) -> HBR-INT-001, HBR-INT-004, HBR-MAN-001, HBR-SWARM-001 = NOT_APPLICABLE for runtime proof on this research/spec-record MT (no product surface, code, test, or runtime state changed by it); their requirement content is recorded for implementation MTs in .GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.192/spec-modules/12-end-of-file-appendices.md#12.7 (HBR applicability row), 10-product-surfaces.md#10.15.8 (UserManual duty) and #10.20 (backend-navigation/parallel-agent receipts); per packet.json acceptance_matrix.note HBR proof is carried per-MT and whole-WP HBR review is the external validator batch pass

concerns: Contract freshness skew: scope.relevant_files and the activation constraint pin the v02.191 bundle while SPEC_CURRENT and all evidence are v02.192 (contracts hydrated 07:20Z after the 06:45Z spec bump); validator should read the constraint intent (indexed bundle, not v02.182 monolith), but a hygiene pass updating contract pins to v02.192 would remove the ambiguity.
adversarial_review.refutation_attempts: Opened every cited artifact (refinement.json, spec-enrichment-proposal.json, stub contract, v02.192 spec modules 02/07/10/11/12, spec-changelog.jsonl, SPEC_CURRENT.md, packet.json); attacked criterion coverage AC1-AC4, citation validity, specificity, v02.192 freshness, and scope-artifact existence. Counted research_basis.sources_checked (exactly 20, names match), verified rejected_options (12 incl. sidecar-paper rejection), the sidecar failure scenario, and native_research_translation_requirements in data_model_seed.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
